content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

March 24, 1992

Steven Dorsey  
Norwalk City Attorney  
c/o Richards, Watson and Gershon  
333 South Hope Street, Thirty-Eighth Floor  
Los Angeles, CA 90071-1469

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-92-089a

Dear Mr. Dorsey:

This is in response to your request for further clarification of the Dorsey Advice Letter, No. A-92-089 which contained advice provided to you on behalf of Councilmember Luigi Vernola concerning his responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

As stated in the Dorsey Advice Letter, Councilmember Vernola would be legally required to participate in the decision to acquire property for a sports arena parking lot where it is impossible to obtain a quorum without his participation. This letter confirms the conclusion in the Dorsey Advice Letter in light of the facts set forth in both your February 4, 1992 letter requesting advice, and your letter of March 16, 1992 seeking further clarification of the advice.

However, please note, as we discussed on March 16, 1992, the advice concerning the property acquisition decision is factually sensitive and was based on the extraordinary circumstances caused by Councilmember White's serious illness. This letter may not be of any precedential value for future decisions where the circumstances may differ.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace  
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa
